content.text:
The issue was caused by `Jsoup.parse(content)` parsing the HTML content as a full document. When the content started with `<script>` tags (or other head elements), Jsoup would place them in the `<head>` of the parsed document. However, `parseSourcesToFileScheme` was returning `doc.body().html()`, which only retrieves the content of the `<body>`, effectively stripping out the `<head>` and thus the `<script>` tags.

The fix involves using `Jsoup.parseBodyFragment(content)` instead. This method parses the input HTML as if it were a fragment inside the `<body>` element, ensuring that all content, including `<script>` tags at the beginning, remains within the body. Consequently, `doc.body().html()` returns the complete content, preserving the scripts while still allowing the image source substitutions to take place.

I verified the fix by creating a test case that reproduced the issue (stripping of `<script>` tags) and confirmed that after the change, the scripts are preserved and image sources are correctly updated.

**Changes:**
Modified `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt`:
- Changed `Jsoup.parse(content)` to `Jsoup.parseBodyFragment(content)` in `parseSourcesToFileScheme`.

```kotlin
fun parseSourcesToFileScheme(content: String, mediaDir: String): String {
    val doc = Jsoup.parseBodyFragment(content)
    doc.outputSettings(OutputSettings().prettyPrint(false))
    // ...
```
